sections.markdown:
- 18 km easy long run.
- Target pace: about `5:55-6:25 /km`, guided by effort and HR rather than forcing pace.
- Start the first `5 km` conservatively around `6:05-6:30 /km` so the run stays genuinely aerobic.
- HR guardrail: keep most of the run in Z2, ideally under about `156 bpm`; brief drift toward `160 bpm` late is acceptable, but do not sit above that early.
- No planned marathon-pace block and no hard fast finish. If the last `3 km` naturally settles around `5:40-5:55 /km` while HR stays controlled, that is fine; otherwise keep it easy.
- Fueling practice: take one gel around `40-45 minutes`, then water. Consider a second gel around `75-80 minutes` if the run will go beyond `1:45`.
- If Saturday basketball was hard or HR drifts above `160 bpm` before `10 km`, shorten to `14-16 km` and preserve the next build week.

- This missed the easy-long-run prescription in both distance and intensity. The plan was `18 km` easy at about `5:55-6:25 /km`; the completed run was `22.367 km` in `1:57:17` moving time and `1:57:21` elapsed, averaging `5:15 /km` with `155.3 bpm` average HR and `181 bpm` max HR.
- It was a strong progressive quality long run, not an easy long run. The first half was controlled at `5:28 /km` and `146.6 bpm`, but the second half accelerated to `5:01 /km` at `164.8 bpm`, with the last `2.354 km` around `4:54 /km` at `171.2 bpm`.

- The week finished at `68.469 km` across five runs against the planned `76 km`. The missing Friday/Saturday easy volume is not the issue; Sunday's extra `4.367 km` and faster second half replaced that missing distance with substantially higher load.
- The later calf tightness and next-day Achilles soreness confirm that this was too costly to follow with the planned `84 km` week. The next few days should absorb the long-run cost before any new threshold or marathon-pace work.

- For the Sydney Marathon `3:30:00` goal, this is an encouraging durability signal: `22.367 km` with a fast close shows the engine is moving toward marathon-specific strength.
- The risk is distribution. The target requires repeatable long runs and marathon-pace work, not turning every long run into a hard progression. This was useful as a quality long run, but the next long run should return closer to the prescribed easy cost unless it is explicitly planned as quality.
- User note added 2026-07-08: "I had some calf tighteness after the sunday long run and then the next day my achilles was a bit sore. It's been progressively getting better."

- Strava moving pace averaged `5:15 /km`, with Strava GAP about `5:12 /km`. The pace progression was clear: `0-5 km` at `5:27 /km`, `5-10 km` at `5:30 /km`, `10-15 km` at `5:05 /km`, `15-20 km` at `5:05 /km`, and `20 km-finish` at `4:54 /km`.
- The final two full kilometres were `4:52 /km` and `4:56 /km`, so the close was marathon-to-half-marathon effort, not a relaxed easy finish.

- Strava HR averaged `155.3 bpm` with a `181 bpm` max; Apple Health matched at about `155.3 bpm` average HR and `181 bpm` max.
- Zone time was mixed rather than easy: about `25:07` in Z1, `36:47` in Z2, `26:36` in Z3, `18:42` in Z4, and `9:41` in Z5. The first half was cheap enough; the second half made this a meaningful quality load.

- Strava moving time was `1:57:17` from `1:57:21` elapsed, only a `0:04` gap.
- The route stream marked `996` of `1000` samples as moving, so the negative split and late HR rise are real execution features.

- Strava logged `103.2 m` elevation gain, while the route stream accumulated about `152.2 m` ascent and `152.6 m` descent.
- GAP was only about `3 s/km` faster than raw pace overall. Terrain mattered a little, but the main story is the deliberate second-half acceleration.

sections.heading: Weather Gear And Data Quality
sections.markdown:
- Conditions were overcast and breezy: about `14.1 C`, `11.9 C` apparent temperature, `64.5%` humidity, no recorded precipitation, `9.9 kph` wind, and `30.6 kph` gusts.
- Shoes were `Adidas Adizero EVO SL Red`. Strava and Apple Health agreed closely, and both sources had streams. Recent Apple Health context showed resting HR falling back to the high `40s` after the run, but sleep was inconsistent across the week; use that as recovery context, not as an excuse for the workout cost.
